analysisQuality.conceptCountPolicy: Return every concept above the minimum teaching threshold. Do not invent concepts to hit a number.
analysisQuality.richnessNotes: The transcript is concept-rich and procedure-heavy. Several items are source-supported but require enrichment or taxonomy review, especially Secure Boot, DiskPart, internet-based installation paths, and modern full-format behavior.

sourceEvidence.quote: One common method is to use a bootable USB drive.
sourceEvidence.reason: Introduces bootable USB media as an OS installation method.

relationshipSuggestions.reason: PXE is used to boot an installer from the network.

conceptsDiscovered.title: Remote network installation from local share

knowledgeGaps.title: Boot process prerequisites are assumed
knowledgeGaps.description: The lesson references booting from USB, PXE, local partitions, ISO images, and firmware settings without fully teaching boot order, firmware setup access, or bootloader behavior.
knowledgeGaps.recommendation: Create or link a supporting boot process/firmware boot order concept before installation media topics.

knowledgeGaps.title: PXE dependencies are not explained
knowledgeGaps.description: PXE is introduced, but DHCP/TFTP or boot image/service dependencies are not explained.
knowledgeGaps.recommendation: Enrich PXE authoring with network service dependencies at an A+ appropriate depth.

knowledgeGaps.title: Data backup risk before destructive installation is implicit
knowledgeGaps.description: Clean installs, repartitioning, and formatting can delete data, but backup procedure and verification are not taught as separate prerequisites.
knowledgeGaps.recommendation: Link to backup and data preservation concepts before destructive install operations.

knowledgeGaps.title: Modern partition terminology needs review
knowledgeGaps.description: The transcript uses 'GID partition table' wording where GUID/GPT terminology should be normalized before authoring.
knowledgeGaps.recommendation: Review and normalize GPT/GUID terminology in authoring output.

knowledgeGaps.title: Format behavior varies by storage type and OS version
knowledgeGaps.description: The quick/full format comparison is exam-relevant, but modern SSD behavior, TRIM, and OS-version differences may need careful enrichment to avoid overgeneralization.
knowledgeGaps.recommendation: Keep A+ baseline clear while adding reviewed caveats if the platform supports deeper notes.

rejectedMentions.title: CD-ROM and DVD-ROM media
rejectedMentions.reason: Optical media is only used as historical context for ISO images and does not exceed the teaching threshold in this lesson.
rejectedMentions.sourceEvidence: Older operating systems used to distribute their code on optical discs.

rejectedMentions.title: External hot-swappable drives
rejectedMentions.reason: Mentioned as possible installation media, but not enough detail is provided beyond media portability.

rejectedMentions.title: Email server settings
rejectedMentions.reason: Used only as an example of corporate configuration in zero-touch deployment, not as an email concept lesson.
rejectedMentions.sourceEvidence: company specific such as email server settings

rejectedMentions.title: Domain connections
rejectedMentions.reason: Mentioned as part of deployment automation, but domain join/identity is not taught here.

rejectedMentions.title: Windows Updates OS installation
rejectedMentions.reason: The source mentions Windows Updates as an internet source without enough detail to classify the exact Windows installation/reset/update mechanism.
rejectedMentions.sourceEvidence: Windows Updates also provides a way to install operating systems directly from an internet source.

importNotes: This output intentionally stays at transcript-intelligence/discovery level and does not create Knowledge Objects.
Several concepts likely already exist from lesson 02 file-system/storage work, especially partition, volume, GPT, MBR, quick format, and full format; review for merge before authoring.
Normalize transcript typos before authoring: PXE not Pixie, multiboot not multioot, zero-touch not zeroouch, reimaging not reiming, GUID not GID.
Secure Boot and DiskPart should probably link to existing concepts rather than be authored from this transcript alone.
